paper_title: Time-dependent fiber bundles with local load sharing
source_file: 机理文献/条件备选/02_Time-dependent fiber bundles with local load sharing.pdf

visual_review.layout_notes: Two-column reading order, equations, Greek symbols, subscripts, inequalities, captions and page numbering were checked against rendered PDF pages.

sections_checked: I.B Overview of paper and main results
II.A Bundle geometry and load sharing rules
III.A Mean and variance of ELS bundle lifetime
III.C Fiber failure rates under ELS for small rho
IV.A LLS bundle lifetime behavior for rho > 1
IV.B LLS bundle lifetime behavior for 0 < rho <= 1
IV.C Fiber failure rates under LLS for small rho
V.A Bundle lifetime behavior for rho > 1
V.B Bundle lifetime behavior for 1/2 <= rho <= 1
V.C Bundle lifetime behavior for 0 <= rho < 1/2
Appendix: Merge-sort algorithm for megafiber bundle simulation
Acknowledgments and references checked for document completeness; not used as primary evidence

equations_checked.notes: Interior LLS factor Kr=1+r/2, boundary factor Kr,b=1+r/2+b/2 and ELS factor N/(N-j) were visually checked. Eq. (2.1) was retained as E1; the other two remain route-card context.
Load-history lifetime distribution, kappa(x)=x^rho, Psi(x)=x^beta, fixed-load Weibull form and cumulative-hazard failure condition were checked. Eq. (2.4)-(2.6) and (2.9) were retained as E2.
ELS failure rates, inter-failure exponential law, mean, variance regimes and Gaussian limit were checked. They are used only as comparison theory and were omitted from the compact formula list.
The exact rho=1 gamma CDF, its mean and variance, Gaussian limit and reverse weakest-link transform were checked. Eq. (3.13) was retained as E7.
Small-rho ELS rate expansions were checked and retained only as support for the low-rho comparison.
k-cluster formation distribution, coefficient recursion, power-law scaling and intersection time were checked. Eq. (4.1)-(4.2) were retained as E4.

Critical-cluster asymptotics, Stirling approximation, k(t), W*(t), critical size, FY propagation approximation and LLS hazard sequence were checked. Omitted to avoid carrying a long derivation; route locations preserve them.

W-hat approximation, constants, propagation-time correction, characteristic failure time and rho=1 cluster recursion were visually checked. Eq. (4.32) was retained as E6; the compact card does not reproduce W-hat because its asymptotic preconditions are restrictive.
Small-rho LLS failure-rate expansions and their ELS comparison were checked and summarized qualitatively in M5.
rho=0 maximum-lifetime extreme-value distribution, median, spread and small-rho conjecture were checked. Omitted as a displayed equation because this is not a target-system calibrated regime.
Projected lifetime contraction after a load change was visually checked, including the ratio ell_i/ell_i' raised to rho. Retained as E3.

Forward/backward pointer definitions, pointer updates, terminal cases and hierarchical minimum/state recursions were checked. Omitted from the compact formula list because they are implementation details rather than physical equations.

figures_checked.reason: Lifetime CDF families for rho=2,4,10,20 are summarized by V1 and the weakest-link formula; exact curve reading is not required for downstream modeling.
Reverse weakest-link transforms and the two asymptotic approximations are adequately indexed by Eq. (4.6), Sections IV.A/V.A and V1.
Mean, standard deviation and coefficient-of-variation size trends are compactly described in the mechanisms and parameter table.
The rho=2 collapse after roughly one-quarter of fibers fail is preserved as a qualified single-realization trend; the plot is replaceable by text.
Gaussian and extreme-value regime comparisons are summarized by V2-V3 without estimating values from plotted curves.
Pointer arrays and binary-tree data structures are implementation diagrams that can be fully specified by the Appendix and do not carry unique physical evidence.

omitted_sections: Broad historical review of composite, fuse-network, percolation and earthquake models in Section I.A
Detailed ELS derivations not needed beyond the comparison and rho=1 benchmark
known_warnings: The model is a one-dimensional fiber abstraction, not a microspine-array geometry or a material calibration experiment.
The nearest-left/right equal LLS rule and open-boundary transfer rule must not be identified with the target array without a mechanical adjacency and stiffness mapping.
All primary simulations use beta=1 and fixed total bundle load; memory effects, displacement control, compliance, re-engagement and multiple failure modes are absent.
Closed-form rho>1 approximations are developed mainly from rho much greater than one and require large N; finite-array use needs direct simulation and sensitivity analysis.
The Mode-I rock exponent range 10-170 appears only as a cited background value on PDF p.3 and is not a parameter measured or validated by this paper.
Monte Carlo agreement verifies the implementation and asymptotics inside the assumed model; it is not physical validation on fibers, rock, brick or microspines.
No figures were extracted because every model-relevant visual was replaceable by equations and qualified text without loss of traceability.
character_count_method: Count of CJK Unified Ideographs in evidence_card.md; equations, Latin identifiers and Markdown syntax excluded from this count.
